tools.name: run_tool
tools.description: Execute a command in REMnux. Supports piped commands (e.g., 'oledump.py sample.doc | grep VBA'). String extraction: For PE files use 'pestr'; for non-PE use 'strings' (ASCII) and 'strings -el' (Unicode). Note: capa matches under namespaces like collection/* or data-manipulation/* can be artifact-level (matched on strings/data) rather than behavioral; a behavioral capability requires the corresponding APIs to be imported or dynamically resolved. analyze_file tags capa findings with evidence_types to make this explicit.

command.description: Command to execute (can include pipes, e.g., 'strings sample.exe | grep -i password')

input_file.description: Input file path (relative to samples dir, or absolute path in local mode) - appended to command

tools.name: get_file_info
tools.description: Get file type, hashes, and basic metadata

file.description: File path relative to samples directory, or absolute path in local mode

tools.name: list_files
tools.description: List files in samples or output directory

tools.name: extract_archive
tools.description: Extract files from a compressed archive (.zip, .7z, .rar), including WinZip AES-256 .zip and header-encrypted .7z (-mhe=on) — these route through 7z automatically. Tries a supplied password first, then common malware passwords (infected, malware, virus) if the archive is password-protected. Returns list of extracted files.

archive_file.description: Path to archive file relative to samples directory (e.g., 'sample.zip')

password.description: Optional password to try first. If not provided, tries common passwords from built-in list.

output_subdir.description: Optional subdirectory name for extracted files. Defaults to archive filename without extension.

tools.name: upload_from_host
tools.description: Upload a file from the host filesystem to the samples directory for analysis. Accepts an absolute host path — the MCP server reads the file locally and transfers it. Maximum file size: 200MB. Files can also be referenced by absolute path in analysis tools, bypassing the need to upload. For files outside the samples directory, pass the full path to get_file_info, analyze_file, or run_tool.

host_path.description: Absolute path to the file on the machine where the MCP server runs (not the remote client in HTTP deployments). When the server is started with --sandbox, the resolved path must reside inside the configured --ingest-root (defaults to the samples directory).

filename.description: Override filename in samples dir (defaults to basename of host_path)

overwrite.description: Whether to overwrite if file exists. Default: false

tools.name: download_from_url
tools.description: Download a file from a URL into the samples directory for analysis. Returns file metadata (hashes, type, size). Supports custom HTTP headers and an optional thug mode for sites requiring JavaScript execution.

url.description: URL to download (http or https only)

filename.description: Override filename in samples dir. If omitted, derived from URL path.

headers.description: Custom HTTP headers as 'Name: value' strings. Example: ['User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0', 'X-Auth-Token: abc123']

method.description: Download method. 'curl' (default) for direct HTTP download. 'thug' for sites requiring JavaScript execution (uses thug honeyclient).

overwrite.description: Whether to overwrite if file exists. Default: false

timeout.description: Download timeout in seconds (default: server timeout)

tools.name: download_file
tools.description: Download a file from the output directory (returns base64-encoded content). Use this to retrieve analysis results. Files are wrapped in a password-protected archive by default to prevent AV/EDR triggers. Pass archive: false for harmless files like text reports. Provide output_path to save directly to the host filesystem.

file_path.description: File path relative to the output directory

output_path.description: Directory on host to save the downloaded file

archive.description: Wrap the file in a password-protected archive before transfer (default: true). Protects against AV/EDR triggers on the host. Pass false for harmless files like text reports.

tools.name: analyze_file
tools.description: Auto-analyze a file using REMnux tools appropriate for the detected file type. Runs `file` to detect type, then executes matching tools (e.g., PE → peframe/capa, PDF → pdfid/pdf-parser, Office → olevba/oleid). Use `depth` to control analysis intensity: 'quick' (triage only), 'standard' (default), 'deep' (includes expensive tools). Note: 'standard' is sufficient for most files; use 'deep' only when standard doesn't reveal enough. Output includes a capability_evidence field (behavior_capable vs artifact_only) and per-capa evidence_types tags so you can tell code-backed capabilities from data-only artifacts — an artifact_only match means the data is present, not that the behavior executes.

file.description: Filename relative to samples directory, or absolute path in local mode

depth.description: Analysis depth. 'quick' (~5-15s): fast triage. 'standard' (~30-90s, default): comprehensive analysis. 'deep' (~2-5min): exhaustive. Use 'deep' only when 'standard' isn't enough.

tools.name: suggest_tools
tools.description: Detect file type and return recommended REMnux analysis tools without executing them. Use this to plan an analysis strategy, then run individual tools with run_tool. Returns tool names, descriptions, depth tiers, and expert analysis hints. For binaries, confirming a behavior (versus merely finding its artifacts) generally requires more than static analysis — plan for emulation (speakeasy) or sandbox detonation when a behavioral claim is needed.

file.description: Filename relative to samples directory, or absolute path in local mode

depth.description: Filter recommendations by depth tier: 'quick' (triage only), 'standard' (default), 'deep' (all tools)

tools.name: extract_iocs
tools.description: Extract IOCs (IPs, domains, URLs, hashes, registry keys, etc.) from text. Pass output from run_tool or analyze_file to identify indicators. Works well with Volatility 3 plugin output (netscan, cmdline, filescan). Returns deduplicated IOCs with confidence scores. Note: an IOC extracted from a binary's strings is an artifact (present in the file) — not evidence the binary uses it at runtime. Cross-reference it against reachable code or dynamic analysis before treating it as an operational indicator.

text.description: Text to extract IOCs from (e.g., output from run_tool or analyze_file)

tools.name: check_behavior_prerequisites
tools.description: Before claiming a Windows PE performs a behavior (clipboard hijacking, HTTP/WinHTTP C2, process injection, registry/LNK persistence, browser-credential theft, screen capture, keylogging, network-share enumeration), check whether the prerequisite APIs are even accessible. Reads the static import table (readpe) and detects packing (diec), then reports a `static_capability` per behavior: capable_statically / incapable_statically / possibly_via_dynamic_resolution (GetProcAddress + loader present) / analysis_incomplete (packed, or a managed/.NET assembly whose native imports don't reflect its capability — don't read it as a clean negative) / not_applicable (not a PE). This is a STATIC gate — it tells you whether the binary CAN call the required APIs, not whether it does. Omit `behavior` to scan all. Confirm any behavior with dynamic analysis.

file.description: Filename relative to the samples directory, or an absolute path in local mode.

behavior.description: Behavior to check (omit to scan ALL known behaviors). One of: clipboard_hijacking, http_c2_wininet, winhttp_c2, socket_c2, process_injection_remote, process_injection_self, registry_persistence_run, lnk_persistence, browser_credential_theft, screen_capture, keylog_polling, keylog_hook, network_share_enum.

tools.name: verify_string_usage
tools.description: Check whether a string embedded in a binary is actually referenced by code, or is a vestigial artifact (e.g. a wallet address or C2 host sitting in .rdata). Uses radare2 to locate the string and find code cross-references to it. Returns a per-match `xref_status`: `referenced_from_code` (an instruction references it) / `no_code_xrefs_detected` (a COMPLETE-analysis null — NOT proof it is unused; the reference may be computed, indirect, or in code the analyzer missed) / `data_only` (non-code file) / `unknown` (analysis incomplete: packed, timed out, or version drift — never a negative). A static check: never concludes a string is 'unused', and confirm runtime use dynamically.

file.description: Filename relative to the samples directory, or an absolute path in local mode.

query.description: The string (or substring) to locate in the binary and cross-reference against code.

depth.description: Analysis depth: 'standard' (radare2 aa; aar — default) or 'deep' (aaa — slower, more thorough).

max_matches.description: Cap on the number of distinct matching strings to classify (default 50).

tools.name: compare_files
tools.description: Compare two related samples (e.g. a loader and its unpacked payload) and return a structured diff: size and entropy deltas, architecture, compiler, packer, imports added/removed, capabilities (capa) added/removed, and section changes. Reuses readpe/diec/capa/radare2. Use depth='quick' to skip the (slower) capa capability diff. Surfaces what each stage adds without re-running tools by hand.

file_a.description: First file (relative to the samples directory, or absolute in local mode).

file_b.description: Second file to compare against file_a.

depth.description: 'standard' (default) includes a capa capability diff (slower — runs capa on both files); 'quick' skips capa (imports/sections/compiler/entropy only).

tools.name: get_tool_help
tools.description: Get usage help for a REMnux tool. Returns the tool's --help output so you can understand available flags, options, and usage patterns.

tool.description: Tool name (e.g., 'capa', 'pdfid.py', 'olevba'). Returns the tool's --help output.

tools.name: check_tools
tools.description: Check which REMnux analysis tools are installed and available. Returns a summary of installed vs missing tools across all file type categories.

tools.name: get_report_template
tools.description: Get a malware analysis report template (Markdown) bundled locally for offline use. Created by Lenny Zeltser, licensed CC BY 4.0. Use it to structure a report after analyzing a sample. The response also carries optional_section_convention: headings marked (Optional) are conditional markers to resolve (include only if warranted, and drop the marker), not literal heading text. For interactive review/scoring or the latest version, the zeltser-website MCP server's malware_get_template offers more when connected.

tools.name: get_report_guidance
tools.description: Get malware analysis report writing guidelines bundled locally for offline use — report sections, required fields, the MBC capability model, ICD-203 confidence, Pyramid-of-Pain IOC tiering, anti-patterns, and review criteria. Use `topic` to narrow the full digest, or topic='triage_checklist' for the pre-claim triage discipline checklist (artifact-vs-behavior gates) to consult at the START of an analysis. Every report-writing response (any topic except 'triage_checklist') also carries optional_section_convention (how to resolve (Optional) section markers when drafting). For interactive review or numeric scoring, the zeltser-website MCP server's malware_review_report / rating_score_writing offer more when connected.

topic.description: Which slice of guidance to return. 'all' (default) returns the full writing-guidelines digest; narrow to 'sections', 'confidence', 'capabilities', 'pyramid_of_pain', 'anti_patterns', 'review', 'writing', 'frameworks', or 'profiles' to reduce size. 'triage_checklist' returns the pre-claim triage discipline checklist (artifact-vs-behavior gates to pass before drawing a behavioral conclusion) — useful at the START of an analysis, not just when writing up.

tools.name: get_osint_guidance
tools.description: OSINT triage for malware indicators. Given the hashes, C2 domains/IPs, and URLs from a sample (for example from analyze_file or extract_iocs), returns malware-specific enrichment tradecraft — hash-first and disclosure-aware, do not tip off the adversary, leads not verdicts — plus a curated catalog of free and freemium lookup services. Use `topic` to pick the guidance slice and `ioc_type` to narrow the catalog to a hash, url, domain, ip, family, or host_artifact. Guidance only: it runs no lookups and stores no API keys; the AI performs the lookups with its own tools.

topic.description: Which slice of guidance prose to return. 'all' (default) returns tradecraft + workflow + access guidance plus a CONDENSED resource catalog. 'tradecraft' returns the OPSEC principles, 'workflow' the per-IOC decision tree, 'access' the free-vs-paid handling, 'resources' the FULL catalog with per-entry detail. Lean flow for a real sample: call once with 'all' for prose plus a condensed index, then 'resources' with an ioc_type per indicator type for detail without re-emitting the prose.

ioc_type.description: Optional. When set, narrows the resource catalog to full-detail entries relevant to that IOC type. Orthogonal to `topic` (which selects the prose): `topic` picks guidance, `ioc_type` picks catalog rows.

resources.name: tools
resources.description: All registered REMnux analysis tools with metadata

resources.name: report-template
resources.description: Malware analysis report template (Markdown, CC BY 4.0, by Lenny Zeltser)

resources.name: report-guidelines
resources.description: Malware analysis report writing guidelines digest (© Lenny Zeltser)

resources.name: report-optional-section-convention
resources.description: How to handle report template headings marked (Optional): conditional markers to resolve, not literal text

resources.name: peframe
resources.description: Statically analyze PE and Microsoft Office files.

resources.name: diec
resources.description: Determine types of files and examine file properties.

resources.name: capa
resources.description: Identify capabilities in executable files using CAPA rules.

resources.name: capa-vv
resources.description: Identify capabilities with verbose rule match details (addresses and evidence).

resources.name: floss
resources.description: Extract and deobfuscate strings from PE executables.

resources.name: pestr
resources.description: Extract ASCII and Unicode strings from PE files with section and offset info.

resources.name: portex
resources.description: Statically analyze PE files for anomalies, structure, and metadata.

resources.name: yara-forge
resources.description: Scan for malware family signatures using curated YARA rules from 45+ sources (Malpedia, ReversingLabs, etc.). Matches indicate resemblance to known families, not confirmed attribution.

resources.name: yara-rules
resources.description: Scan a file with YARA rules to identify capabilities and behaviors (packer detection, anti-debug, networking).

resources.name: pecheck
resources.description: Analyze static properties of PE files.

resources.name: disitool
resources.description: Examine and manipulate embedded Authenticode digital signatures in PE files.

resources.name: pescan
resources.description: Scan PE files for anomalies and notable indicators.

resources.name: signsrch
resources.description: Find patterns of common encryption, compression, or encoding algorithms.

resources.name: ilspycmd
resources.description: Decompile .NET assemblies to C# source code.

resources.name: monodis-presources
resources.description: List embedded manifest resources in a .NET assembly (names and offsets).

resources.name: monodis-mresources
resources.description: Extract all embedded managed resources from a .NET assembly to the current directory.

resources.name: autoit-ripper
resources.description: Extract and decompile AutoIt scripts from compiled executables.

resources.name: upx-decompress
resources.description: Decompress UPX-packed executables in-place (keeps backup as .exe~).

resources.name: manalyze
resources.description: Statically analyze PE files for imports, resources, and anomalies.
Analyze Cobalt Strike beacons and extract configuration details.

resources.name: cs-decrypt-metadata
resources.description: Decrypt and analyze Cobalt Strike beacon metadata.

resources.name: csce
resources.description: Extract Cobalt Strike beacon configuration from raw shellcode or memory dumps.

resources.name: pedump
resources.description: Statically analyze PE files and extract their components.

resources.name: dotnetfile_dump
resources.description: Analyze static properties of .NET files.

resources.name: brxor
resources.description: Bruteforce XOR-encoded strings to find English words.

resources.name: pdfid
resources.description: Identify notable elements of a PDF file.

resources.name: pdfid-detailed
resources.description: Identify notable elements of a PDF file (detailed names output).

resources.name: pdf-parser
resources.description: Examine elements and structure of a PDF file.

resources.name: pdftool
resources.description: Analyze incremental updates in PDF files to detect hidden payload swaps.

resources.name: pdfresurrect
resources.description: Extract previous versions of content from PDF files.

resources.name: peepdf-3
resources.description: Examine elements of a PDF file for notable content.

resources.name: qpdf
resources.description: Decrypt password-protected or permission-locked PDF files.

resources.name: pdftk
resources.description: Manipulate PDF files: merge, split, decrypt, repair, and extract metadata.

resources.name: pdfcop
resources.description: Detect malicious PDF structures using policy-based heuristics.

resources.name: pdfextract
resources.description: Extract JavaScript, attachments, fonts, images, and streams from PDF.

resources.name: pdfdecompress
resources.description: Decompress PDF streams to reveal obfuscated content.

resources.name: oleid
resources.description: Analyze OLE2 files for risk indicators (macros, encryption, etc.).

resources.name: olevba
resources.description: Extract and analyze VBA macros from Microsoft Office documents.

resources.name: oledump
resources.description: Analyze OLE2 Structured Storage files.

resources.name: msoffcrypto-crack
resources.description: Attempt to recover the password of encrypted Microsoft Office documents.

resources.name: pcodedmp
resources.description: Disassemble VBA p-code from Office documents.

resources.name: xlmdeobfuscator
resources.description: Deobfuscate Excel 4.0 (XLM) macros.

resources.name: zipdump
resources.description: Analyze zip-compressed files including OOXML and JAR.

resources.name: xmldump
resources.description: Format and analyze XML. For OOXML: zipdump.py -s <n> -d file | xmldump.py pretty

resources.name: rtfdump
resources.description: Analyze RTF files for embedded content.

resources.name: rtfobj
resources.description: Extract embedded objects from RTF files.

resources.name: redress
resources.description: Analyze Go binaries to recover package names, type definitions, source structure, and compiler version.

resources.name: r2ghidra
resources.description: Decompile native PE/ELF functions to pseudo-C using the r2ghidra (Ghidra) decompiler in radare2. Function-scoped — one function per 'pdg'. List functions first: run_tool command="r2 -A -q -c afl <file>", then decompile a target: run_tool command="r2 -A -q -c 'pdg @ main' <file>" ('pdg @ entry0', 'pdg @ sym.<name>', or 'pdg @ 0x<addr>'). If the Ghidra plugin is unavailable, use radare2's native 'pdc' instead of 'pdg' (lower fidelity, no plugin). Decompiled output can be fed to extract_iocs.

resources.name: decai
resources.description: AI-assisted decompiler: turn a native PE/ELF function into readable pseudo-C with the decai radare2 plugin. REQUIRES a configured LLM backend — an API key or a local Ollama, set via 'decai -e' (e.g. 'decai -e api=openai'); without one it returns an error, not code. Function-scoped: first list functions with run_tool command="r2 -A -q -c afl <file>", then decompile a target by seeking to it: run_tool command="r2 -A -q -c 's main; decai -d' <file>" (or 's sym.<name>;', 's 0x<addr>;'). Check availability and settings with get_tool_help tool="decai". For deterministic, offline decompilation that needs no API key, use r2ghidra (pdg) instead.

resources.name: r2ai
resources.description: AI assistant for radare2 (r2ai plugin): decompile or ask natural-language questions about the current function. REQUIRES a configured LLM backend — an API key or a local Ollama; without one it returns an error. Seek to a function first, then: run_tool command="r2 -A -q -c 's main; r2ai -d' <file>" to decompile, or run_tool command="r2 -A -q -c 's main; r2ai -d what does this function do' <file>" to ask about it. Check availability and settings with get_tool_help tool="r2ai". For deterministic, offline decompilation that needs no API key, use r2ghidra (pdg) instead.

resources.name: readelf-header
resources.description: Display ELF file header information.

resources.name: readelf-sections
resources.description: Display ELF section headers.

resources.name: js-beautify
resources.description: Beautify and deobfuscate JavaScript, CSS, and HTML files.

resources.name: webcrack
resources.description: Deobfuscate, unminify, and unpack bundled JavaScript, including scripts protected with obfuscator.io. Prints deobfuscated code to stdout; with -o <dir> it writes <dir>/deobfuscated.js instead, but refuses to run if the directory already exists — point -o at a fresh path and never pre-create it.

resources.name: box-js
resources.description: Emulate JavaScript in a sandbox to observe runtime behavior — recovers URLs and config assembled at runtime that static deobfuscation (webcrack) leaves unresolved. An older, emulation-based tool: it stalls on anti-emulation (e.g. a wscript self-relaunch), so it is a deliberate deep-tier step, not part of the fast standard chain.

resources.name: jstillery
resources.description: Deobfuscate JavaScript using AST-based partial evaluation.

resources.name: spidermonkey
resources.description: Execute JavaScript with SpiderMonkey engine using browser/PDF object emulation.

resources.name: js-deobfuscator
resources.description: Deobfuscate JavaScript by removing string arrays, proxy functions, and other common obfuscation.

resources.name: gootloader-decode
resources.description: Statically deobfuscate GootLoader JScript to recover the payload and extract C2 domains.

resources.name: strings
resources.description: Extract printable ASCII strings from binary files. For Unicode (UTF-16), use 'strings -e l <file>' (little-endian) or 'strings -e b <file>' (big-endian). For PE files, prefer pestr which extracts both automatically.

resources.name: decode-vbe
resources.description: Decode VBE-encoded VBScript files to readable source.

resources.name: base64dump
resources.description: Locate and decode Base64 and other encoded strings.

resources.name: pycdc
resources.description: Decompile Python bytecode (.pyc) to readable source code.

resources.name: uncompyle6
resources.description: Decompile Python bytecode (.pyc) to source code. Supports Python 1.0 through 3.8.

resources.name: pyinstxtractor-ng
resources.description: Extract contents of PyInstaller executables without requiring a matching Python version.

resources.name: cfr
resources.description: Decompile Java class files and JARs to readable Java source code.

resources.name: emldump

resources.name: msgconvert

resources.name: onedump

resources.name: droidlysis

resources.name: jadx

resources.name: numbers-to-string

resources.name: re-search

resources.name: file-magic

resources.name: cut-bytes

resources.name: format-bytes

resources.name: xor-kpa
resources.description: Perform XOR decryption using known-plaintext attack.

resources.name: sets
resources.description: Perform set operations (union, intersection, difference) on text file lines or bytes.

resources.name: speakeasy-sc-x86
resources.description: Emulate 32-bit shellcode using Speakeasy Windows API emulation.

resources.name: speakeasy-sc-x64
resources.description: Emulate 64-bit shellcode using Speakeasy Windows API emulation.

resources.name: speakeasy
resources.description: Emulate Windows PE, DLL, and driver execution using Speakeasy API emulation.

resources.name: qltool-sc-x86
resources.description: Emulate 32-bit Windows shellcode using Qiling framework (requires rootfs).

resources.name: qltool-sc-x64
resources.description: Emulate 64-bit Windows shellcode using Qiling framework (requires rootfs).

resources.name: tracesc
resources.description: Execute and trace shellcode via Wine to log API calls and behavior.

resources.name: capinfos
resources.description: Capture overview: packet count, duration, start/end timestamps, data rates, and link-layer encapsulation for a PCAP file.

resources.name: tshark-conversations
resources.description: Summarize TCP/UDP conversations from a PCAP file.

resources.name: tshark-http
resources.description: Show HTTP request/response statistics from a PCAP file.

resources.name: tshark-hierarchy
resources.description: Show protocol hierarchy statistics from a PCAP file.

resources.name: tshark-dns
resources.description: Extract DNS queries and responses from a PCAP file.

resources.name: tshark-tls
resources.description: Extract TLS Client Hello server names (SNI) — surfaces destination/C2 domains even when payloads are encrypted.

resources.name: tshark-fingerprint
resources.description: Fingerprint TLS clients (JA4 and JA3) per Client Hello, with SNI and destination IP/port, to cluster traffic by client stack and pivot to threat intel. Uses tshark's native fields, no plugin.

resources.name: tshark-verbose
resources.description: Full packet decode of first 50 packets from a PCAP file.

resources.name: ssdeep
resources.description: Compute fuzzy hash (ssdeep) for file similarity comparison.

resources.name: exiftool
resources.description: Read and analyze EXIF metadata from various file types.

resources.name: xorsearch
resources.description: Locate and decode strings obfuscated using XOR and other techniques.

resources.name: xorsearch.py
resources.description: Search for XOR/ROL/ROT/SHIFT encoded strings with JSON output and YARA support.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 16 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Server accepts path traversal cursor without validation
findings.description: tools/list accepted cursor='../../../../etc/passwd' and returned a result instead of rejecting it. This may indicate missing cursor validation.
findings.remediation: Validate cursor values; reject non-opaque or path-like cursor strings.

findings.title: Server correctly ignores cancellation of unknown request
findings.description: The server remained responsive after receiving notifications/cancelled for a non-existent request.

findings.title: Cancellation race condition timing detected — server responded normally
findings.description: The request/cancel race did not cause a hang; the server responded to one of the two messages.
findings.remediation: No action required — server handled the race gracefully.
